text: When working with APIs, you typically send HTTP requests to a server and receive data in JSON format. First, you need to obtain an API key for authentication, which proves you have permission to access the service. Most APIs have rate limits that restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period—exceeding these may temporarily block your access. It is essential to include error handling in your code, because requests can fail due to network issues or invalid parameters. By checking the response status codes, you can manage errors gracefully and ensure your application runs smoothly.
